userPrompt:
Project context: # Wellframe (Working Title)

## Product Requirements Document (PRD)

**Version:** 1.0
**Platform:** Desktop (macOS first, Windows/Linux later) + lightweight iOS companion app
**Tech Stack:** Tauri (preferred) or Electron + React + TypeScript

---

# Vision

Build the most beautiful, privacy-first AI wellness application available.

Unlike existing health dashboards that lock users into ecosystems or require uploading personal data to the cloud, Wellframe is a local-first desktop application that gives users complete ownership of their health information while providing intelligent coaching powered by whichever AI model they choose.

The application should feel less like a medical record and more like having a thoughtful performance coach sitting beside you throughout the day.

Everything should prioritize:

* Privacy
* Calmness
* Simplicity
* Beautiful design
* User control
* Long-term insights over short-term notifications

The application should feel inspired by companies like Vercel, Linear, Raycast, Arc, and Notion rather than traditional healthcare software.

---

# Design Principles

## Local First

The user's data belongs to them.

The application should function without creating an account.

No backend should be required for the core experience.

Users should be able to:

* Export everything
* Delete everything
* Inspect everything sent to AI
* Choose their AI provider
* Run entirely offline with local models if desired

---

## AI Is A Coach, Not A Chatbot

The AI should understand context.

Instead of waiting for questions, it should proactively identify trends.

Examples:

"I noticed your resting heart rate has increased for four days while your sleep has decreased."

"You've consistently run better after taking Wednesdays as recovery days."

"Your long runs appear to recover faster when protein intake exceeds 120g."

The coach should feel observant rather than verbose.

---

## Beautiful Software

The interface should feel like premium productivity software.

Goals:

* minimal
* spacious
* typography-first
* fast
* elegant
* delightful animations
* subtle motion
* tasteful gradients
* extremely polished

Never feel clinical.

---

# Target Users

Primary:

* runners
* cyclists
* climbers
* lifters
* hikers
* busy professionals
* founders
* engineers
* quantified-self enthusiasts

Secondary:

* casual fitness users
* Apple Health users
* Garmin users
* Strava users
* Oura users

---

# Core Value Proposition

Bring together all of your health data into one beautiful local dashboard with an AI coach that actually understands your long-term health.

---

# Core Features

## Dashboard

Landing page.

Contains:

Today's readiness score

Sleep summary

Recovery summary

Steps

Active minutes

Today's workouts

Weather

Calendar integration (optional)

Upcoming race goals

Suggested workout

Coach summary

Recent trends

The dashboard should immediately answer:

"How am I doing today?"

---

## Activity Timeline

Chronological timeline showing:

Runs

Walks

Bike rides

Strength workouts

Yoga

Stretching

Recovery

Journal entries

Mood

Meals

Body weight

Photos

Notes

Everything should be filterable.

---

## Trends

Weekly

Monthly

Yearly

Examples:

Average sleep

HRV

Resting HR

Mileage

VO2 Max

Training load

Consistency

Step averages

Sleep debt

Recovery

Nutrition

Body weight

Mood

Stress

Custom charts.

---

## AI Coach

The centerpiece of the application.

Should understand:

Goals

Injuries

Recent workouts

Recovery

Sleep

Stress

Habits

Travel

Weather

Calendar

Examples:

"What should I do today?"

"Am I overtraining?"

"Why am I more tired lately?"

"Prepare me for my trail race."

"What patterns are you seeing?"

"How can I sleep better?"

"Why did my pace suddenly improve?"

The AI should use structured tools rather than relying entirely on prompt engineering.

---

## Daily Check-in

Morning

Energy

Mood

Sleep quality

Soreness

Stress

Motivation

Evening

Reflection

Wins

Challenges

Nutrition

Recovery

Notes

Everything becomes searchable.

---

## Goals

Examples:

Run 500 miles this year

Complete marathon

Improve VO2 Max

Sleep 8 hours

Walk 10,000 steps

Strength train 3x/week

Drink more water

Meditate daily

Custom goals.

---

## Journal

Markdown editor.

Supports:

photos

voice transcription

tags

AI summaries

daily prompts

search

linked workouts

---

## Recovery Center

Combines:

Sleep

HRV

RHR

Training Load

Stress

Fatigue

Mood

Suggests:

Recovery runs

Rest day

Mobility

Stretching

Walks

Hydration

Nutrition

---

## Health Vault

Every piece of imported data.

User can:

browse

edit

delete

export

merge

inspect

Nothing hidden.

---

## AI Prompt Inspector

Every AI request shows:

Context included

Prompt

Tool outputs

Response

Estimated cost

Model used

Privacy warnings

Allows editing before sending.

---

# Integrations

## Apple Health

Requires iOS companion application.

Imports:

Steps

Sleep

Heart rate

Resting HR

HRV

VO2 Max

Weight

Workouts

Energy

Flights climbed

Walking steadiness

Blood oxygen

Respiration

Mindfulness

Mobility metrics

Cycle tracking (optional)

---

## Strava

OAuth.

Import:

Activities

Routes

Gear

Photos

PRs

Splits

Elevation

Segments

---

## Garmin

Future.

---

## Oura

Future.

---

## WHOOP

Future.

---

## Polar

Future.

---

## Fitbit

Future.

---

## CSV Import

Any spreadsheet.

---

# AI Providers

Pluggable architecture.

Supports:

OpenAI

Anthropic Claude

Google Gemini

Ollama

LM Studio

Future providers via plugins.

Users bring their own API keys.

---

# Local AI

Support:

Ollama

LM Studio

Future MCP-compatible local models

No cloud required.

---

# Architecture

Desktop

Tauri

React

TypeScript

Vite

TanStack Router

TanStack Query

Tailwind

shadcn/ui

Motion

SQLite

SQLCipher

React Hook Form

Zod

React Markdown

Chart.js or Observable Plot

---

# Storage

Encrypted SQLite.

Tables include:

activities

sleep

nutrition

biometrics

heart_rate

hrv

goals

journal

coach_sessions

mood

integrations

settings

weights

injuries

attachments

ai_history

Everything indexed.

---

# Coach Architecture

Context Builder

↓

Relevant History Retrieval

↓

Tool Execution

↓

Prompt Generation

↓

AI Provider

↓

Structured Output

↓

Coach UI

Tools include:

Analyze sleep

Analyze trends

Retrieve workouts

Summarize journal

Compute training load

Estimate fatigue

Recommend recovery

Find similar historical weeks

Generate plan

---

# Privacy Features

Local-first

Encrypted database

No required account

Offline mode

Prompt inspector

Per-provider permissions

Export everything

Delete everything

Never send raw health data mode

Summary-only AI mode

PII redaction

---

# Themes

Light

Dark

High Contrast

System

---

# Design Language

Inspired by:

Vercel

Linear

Raycast

Arc

Geist

Notion

Apple Human Interface Guidelines

Characteristics:

Large typography

Rounded cards

Thin borders

Soft shadows

Elegant spacing

Minimal color

Motion with purpose

Excellent empty states

Keyboard-first interactions

Command palette

Beautiful charts

Subtle gradients

---

# Keyboard Shortcuts

⌘K

Open command palette

⌘J

Open journal

⌘/

Ask coach

⌘1-8

Navigate sections

Space

Quick add

---

# Nice-to-Have Features

Weather-aware coaching

Travel mode

Timezone adjustment

Menstrual cycle insights

Nutrition logging

Recipe recommendations

Apple Calendar integration

Google Calendar integration

Habit tracking

Medication reminders

Hydration tracking

Mood visualization

Export to Markdown

Weekly PDF summaries

Home screen widgets

Desktop widgets

Menu bar app

Apple Watch companion

---

# Future AI Features

Voice conversations

Real-time coaching

Meeting-aware recommendations

Screen-aware coaching

Daily briefing

Weekly review

Monthly review

Training plan generation

Race prediction

Recovery forecasting

Habit prediction

Long-term health trend detection

Anomaly detection

Personal knowledge graph

Memory across years

Natural-language analytics

---

# Stretch Vision

Wellframe becomes the operating system for personal health.

Instead of dozens of disconnected health applications, wearable dashboards, and AI chats, users have one trusted place where every piece of health information lives, every trend is understandable, and every recommendation is personalized.

The application should become more valuable every day it is used, learning from years of health history while always keeping that history under the user's control.

The experience should feel like the intersection of Apple Health, Strava, Oura, Notion, and ChatGPT—reimagined as a beautiful, local-first desktop application built for people who care deeply about understanding themselves.
